plan_section: Step 2: Colorado Mesh assets and visual system handoff

plan_alignment.notes: The previous asset wiring blocker is resolved: shared brand constants, BrandMark, layout metadata, manifest icons, and app favicon now point to vendored Colorado Mesh assets under /public/brand.

correctness_safety.notes: The previous mobile-menu focus blocker is resolved with pointer-events-none, aria-hidden, and inert while closed. Navigation avoids observer as a primary item and includes the planned routes.

code_quality.notes: BrandMark now derives display text and logo path from shared constants. The visual primitives are typed, cohesive React components with no data-fetching side effects.

completeness.notes: Step 2 shell branding, reusable primitives, icon references, and mobile menu semantics are complete for this stage.

patterns.notes: Navigation, footer, metadata, and brand mark follow the centralized constants pattern introduced by the plan.

integration.notes: Referenced public asset paths exist, staged asset file types match their use, and no prototype runtime artifacts were found in the reviewed shell files.

nonblocking_issues.note: Clicking the BrandMark inside the open mobile menu may navigate without explicitly calling onClose; consider adding close behavior if route transitions preserve the shell state.

flags.note: Clicking the BrandMark inside the open mobile menu may navigate without explicitly calling onClose; consider adding close behavior if route transitions preserve the shell state.

verification_notes: Implementer-reported lint, typecheck, build, grep, diff check, and browser validation are aligned with the Step 2 verification plan.
Review confirmed staged paths now wire active branding to /brand assets and that the previously reported closed-menu focusability issue has been addressed.
Review did not independently rerun the full npm verification suite.
summary: Previous blocking issues are resolved and no new blocking correctness, security, performance, or plan-alignment issues were found. Step 2 is approved.
